title: Cursor vs. Copilot vs. Codeium — The Real Winner in 2025
author: Abhinav
text:
Member-only story
Cursor vs. Copilot vs. Codeium — The Real Winner in 2025
So I’m sitting here at 2 AM, staring at a bug that’s been haunting me for three hours. My coffee’s gone cold, again, and I’m switching between three different AI coding tools like I’m channel surfing.
Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Codeium. They’re all promising to save my life, but honestly? I just want something that works without making me feel stupid.
I’ve been wrestling with all three for the past few months on actual projects. Not toy examples or hello world apps. Real messy, deadline-driven, “why did the previous dev write it this way” kind of projects.
Here’s what I learned.
How They Actually Feel
You know that moment when you’re stuck on a problem and you wish you could just ask someone who knows what they’re doing?
Cursor is like having that senior dev who actually remembers what you talked about yesterday. You paste some code, ask “what’s wrong here,” and it gives you an answer that makes sense. It knows your project, remembers your patterns, gets the context. But man, it’s not cheap.
GitHub Copilot feels like your coding buddy who’s always ready with a quick suggestion. Not always genius-level, but reliable. Works everywhere. Never gives you attitude. Just quietly does its…
